items.title: Darwin vacation care worker charged with indecently touching child

children.content: NT Police allege the male educator, 40, indecently touched a child under the age of 16 at a vacation care program in Darwin.

image.altText: An NT Police Car parked on the street.

items.title: A speeding shadow minister puts the brakes on NT Labor’s rebuild

children.content: Revelations about an NT shadow minister’s dicey driving history — and the Opposition Leader's failure to disclose it — come as another blow to NT Labor.

image.altText: an aboriginal man wearing a suit in parliament

items.title: While teaching about pancreatic cancer, John realised he ticked every box

children.content: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

gallery.altText: A close-up portrait of a bald man looking straight at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A close-up portrait of a bald man looking straight at the camera

items.title: Former cricketer pleads guilty to underage sexual abuse charges

children.content: Aaron William Summers was playing for the Hobart Hurricanes in January 2018 when the underage sexual abuse occurred, a Tasmanian court has heard.

image.altText: A man in a white hoodie and baseball cap walking

items.title: Millions spent on hardline school policies, but NT students not turning up

children.content: Just over half of all Aboriginal students in the NT are showing up for school, despite millions being spent on truancy policies.

image.altText: A group of primary aged kids in school uniforms walking on a red dirt road.

items.title: Australia's next opponent locked in after World Cup chaos

children.content: The Socceroos will meet Egypt in the knockout stage of the World Cup after a stunning finish in Group G. Follow live.

articles.title: Egypt and Iran push back against 'Pride Match' as rainbow flags wave

children.content: Rainbow flags wave around Seattle after Egypt and Iran asked FIFA to keep a Pride celebration out of its World Cup match.

image.altText: A woman ina pride flag stands outside a stadium.

articles.title: Live table: Which third-placed teams will make it to the knockouts?

children.content: The World Cup group stage is winding down, with third-placed teams anxiously watching other results to learn their fate. See the live table to see who is on track to progress.

image.altText: The Socceroos huddle in a circle

articles.title: Departure of Leckie and Italiano leaves Socceroos 'two soldiers down'

children.content: Australia enters the World Cup knockout rounds with just 24 players in the squad after veteran Mathew Leckie and right-back Jacob Italiano departed the camp with injuries.

image.altText: A soccer player is treated for an injury

items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

image.altText: birds eye view of a bright pink lake, with a large body of blue water in the background

items.title: Crime scene declared after armed man shot by police south of Brisbane

children.content: An armed man has been shot by police south of Brisbane after he allegedly confronted officers while carrying a rifle.

image.altText: Police tape in focus in front of a cream and blue house.

items.title: In Beirut, a sacred ceremony also becomes display of Hezbollah's force

children.content: Tens of thousands of Shiite Muslims gathered in Beirut for the largest Ashura procession in years, as Hezbollah used the holy day to project strength despite growing pressure to disarm after months of war with Israel.

gallery.altText: Dozens of men, sitting cross-legged, all of them wearing black. Some are wearing white turbans.

thumbnail.altText: Dozens of men, sitting cross-legged, all of them wearing black. Some are wearing white turbans.

items.title: More rain on the way for WA after storm damages roofs, downs trees

children.content: Perth residents are bracing for more wet weather after a powerful cold front damaged roofs and downed trees on Friday night.

image.altText: A tree lies across the roof of a house with SES crews in attendance

items.title: 'Among the darkest hours of my life': Pete Buttigieg victim of false report

children.content: High profile Democrat Pete Buttigieg has been the victim of a false anonymous report, alleging he was a 'danger' to his four-year-old twins.

image.altText: Pete Buttigieg speaking into a microphone.

items.title: Heartbreaking scenes emerging from earthquakes in Venezuela

children.content: After two powerful earthquakes, rescue teams from around the globe are in a race against time to pull survivors from the rubble.

articles.title: Why Venezuela doesn't have an 'earthquake preparedness culture'

children.content: As a rare one-two punch of earthquakes hit Venezuela this week, residents in the capital Caracas watched in horror as buildings shook and were sent "pancaking" before their eyes.

image.altText: A man involved in rescue efforts looks on from a damaged building in Venezuela.

items.title: Magpies face struggling Tigers as Blues celebrate sixth straight win

children.content: Carlton makes it six straight wins under interim coach Josh Fraser with a big victory over West Coast, ahead of the 60th edition of the Showdown in Adelaide tonight. Follow live.

image.altText: Nick Daicos pumps his fist

items.title: Dolphins win NRL epic after star half suffers dislocated wrist

children.content: The Dolphins have overcome the loss of their halfback with a wrist dislocation to defeat the New Zealand Warriors 26-24. Follow live.

image.altText: Jamayne Isaako of the Dolphins scores a try, diving in the air and grounding with one hand

items.title: John Williamson nicked a melody from a butcher bird

children.content: Now that folk and country music legend John Williamson is 80, he wants to spend less time touring and more time at home eating his own tomatoes.

items.title: Photographer captures elders' stories and WA's stunning southern landscapes

items.title: A warm and comforting gnocchi bake

children.content: "This would be something I'd love to make on a Sunday and have it bubbling away while you're doing odd jobs around the house."

items.title: Shock results from peptide test reveal 'life-threatening' ingredients

children.content: Doctors warn of an "enormous" risk of overdose after testing of a popular unapproved weight loss peptide reveals the product had double the strength of what was listed on its label.

image.altText: A vial of liquid sitting on a table with a label saying 'retatrutide 10mg'

items.title: Described as a 'talented athlete', Hussein Dehaini was gunned down at 33

children.content: Those who knew Hussein Dehaini say he could have been anything he wanted to be. But his life ended at 33, when he was gunned down in the middle of a Melbourne street.

image.altText: Hussein Dehaini pictured in a gym wearing shorts and no top

items.title: Thousands in south-east Qld without power after attempted copper theft

children.content: Energex says it appears someone broke into a substation's cable pit and attempted to cut through an 11,000-volt cable, leaving about 13,000 south-east Queensland customers without power overnight.

image.altText: Kev Lavender substation

items.title: Defence home loan subsidy to change after veterans left worse off

children.content: Critics say the scheme, which offers subsidised home loans, lumps Defence members and veterans with uncompetitive rates.

image.altText: Medals pinned to a soldier's uniform.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Bedlam in Group H, Dua Lipa surprise and superstars warm the bench

children.content: Group H delivers us incredible drama, some of the World Cup's biggest stars find themselves warming the pine, and Dua Lipa makes a surprise appearance. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: 'Entitled to dream': Cabo Verde's World Cup run inspires the nation

children.content: Cabo Verde has secured the most unlikely spot in the World Cup knockouts, and the nation of 500,000 people is daring to dream.

articles.title: Dembélé suspends time as France's favouritism heightens

items.title: Sect leader convicted after girls found in trailer on US highway

items.title: Why does Perth have an obsession with double brick homes unlike any other city?

children.content: It takes 12 to 18 months on average to build a home in Western Australia — but with non-brick frame construction, the home building timeline could be cut by up to half.

image.altText: A couple smile to the camera at a house construction site.

items.title: Should dementia patients be able to choose to die?

children.content: Families and dementia patients say they should be able to die on their own terms, so why isn't dementia treated like other incurable diseases? What would it take to change Voluntary Assisted Dying laws?

items.title: Olive industry 'buzzing' as diets fuel demand, farmers diversify

children.content: Consumer interest in olive oil has boomed at the same time as growing the produce has flourished, leading to excitement in the industry.
